# Copilot Instructions — House Rules

Project notes, architecture, build, RE resources, and conventions live in
[`CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Read it first. This file only adds guidance
specific to Copilot / multi-agent workflows.

## Read before editing

1. `CLAUDE.md` — architecture, v0.1 scope, v0.2 plan, conventions.
2. `.local/findings/` — prior research on vanilla internals. Don't redo work
   that's already documented there.
3. `.local/design/` — in-flight design notes (e.g. `HCManagerProbe` plan).

## Current shape (quick reference)

- Core ships as `HouseRules.dll` + `HouseRules.esp`. No fork of
  `Hardcore:HC_ManagerScript`. Survival-needs work, if it ships, lands in an
  optional `HouseRulesSurvival.esp` + script module — not in core.
- **v0.2 core plan**: Magnitudes log cleanup + Difficulty I GMST sliders
  (incoming damage, outgoing damage, XP rate, legendary chance/rarity).
- **Survival next step**: `HCManagerProbe` (dev-only VM read probe) before
  any core onset writer.

## Copilot / agent rules

- **Stay in scope.** One task per agent invocation. Do not refactor unrelated
  areas or rename existing modules while doing a feature.
- **Don't fight other agents.** Never edit a file another agent is currently
  editing. The orchestrator serializes per-file work; if you're not sure, ask
  or pick a different file.
- **Findings → `.local/`.** Investigation write-ups go in `.local/design/` or
  `.local/findings/`. Do not create broad planning markdown at the repo root.
  The only agent-facing docs in-repo are `CLAUDE.md` and this file.
- **Session/LLM artifacts → `.claude/`.** Keep them out of normal commits.
- **No feature branches.** Personal repo, stay on `master`.
- **No AI attribution in commits.** No `Co-authored-by: Copilot` or similar
  trailers, no "generated by" notes in messages.
- **Verify hooks.** After adding/retargeting a hook, run
  `tools/audit_hook_offsets.py` against the NG fixture before claiming done.

## Local reference paths (verify on each machine)

- `.local/` — gitignored project notes & installed reference mods.
- `../Fallout4RE/` — sibling RE workspace (`Scratch/`, `Targets/`, `Workspace/`).
- `../RE/` — *user-suggested*, not currently present here.
- `C:/Utilities/ReverseEngineering/` — DIA2Dump, Ghidra, Steamless, x64dbg.
- `C:/Games/Modding/Nucleus/mods/` — unpacked game exes, AddressLib bins,
  installed reference mods.

See `CLAUDE.md` → "Reverse-engineering resources" for exact paths and tool
invocations.
